title: Core Concepts of Computation Theory (Ullman)
description: A flowchart illustrating the hierarchy and relationships between fundamental concepts in computation theory as presented by Jeffrey D. Ullman.
relationships: Finite Automata recognize Regular Languages described by Regular Expressions
Turing Machines are the ultimate model of computation capable of simulating all others
Decidability defines what problems Turing Machines can solve
Computability Theory studies the limits of what can be computed
Complexity Classes categorize problems by the resources (time/space) required
